summary.brief: Faiaipau v. THC-Orange County, LLC. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: Ana was born in 1944. She underwent heart surgery in January 2023 and was placed on a ventilator and feeding tube while she recovered.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In March 2023, Ana was transferred to Kindred, a long-term acute care hospital, for care during her recovery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Kindred withheld dialysis treatment from Ana, which led to swelling, discomfort, and harm to her health.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ana’s ventilator began beeping and flashing red while her family was visiting her. No staff responded until Ana’s family yelled and shouted for help.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ana had suffered a severe anoxic brain injury as a result of the disconnection of the ventilator.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court’s order is modified to compel arbitration of Jennifer and Faamalieloto’s claim for wrongful death, as well as the negligence, neglect, fraud, and UCL claims brought on Ana’s behalf.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Because Kindred’s alleged failure to monitor Ana’s ventilator that led to her death involved medical and not custodial care, the trial court erred by not ordering Jennifer and Faamalieloto’s wrongful death claim based on the ventilator to arbitration.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: Kindred shall recover its costs on appeal.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Recovery from heart surgery with ventilator support
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Ana underwent heart surgery in January 2023 and was placed on a ventilator and feeding tube while she recovered. In March 2023, she was transferred to Kindred, a long-term acute care hospital, for continued care.

requestables.name: Respiratory Care Plan
requestables.description: Plan for weaning off ventilator and monitoring

reveal.clinical_significance: Critical for ensuring proper ventilator management
reveal.decision_impact: Should guide monitoring and intervention strategies

requestables.name: Ventilator Monitoring
requestables.description: Documentation of ventilator checks and alarms

reveal.result_summary: Ventilator alarms not responded to in a timely manner
reveal.clinical_significance: Failure to respond led to anoxic brain injury
reveal.decision_impact: Critical failure in monitoring leading to severe outcome

timeline_phases.name: Transfer to Long-term Acute Care
timeline_phases.description: Patient transferred to Kindred for continued recovery

timeline_phases.name: Ventilator Monitoring
timeline_phases.description: Period during which ventilator monitoring was critical

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in long-term acute care with ventilator support
decision_points.prompt: How should the ventilator be monitored?

options.description: Implement regular checks and immediate response to alarms
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care requires continuous monitoring of ventilator function

options.description: Rely on periodic checks without immediate response to alarms
options.clinical_reasoning: Inadequate monitoring can lead to critical failures

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant failed to monitor ventilator adequately

expected_action_court.description: Continuous monitoring and immediate response to alarms
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established this as standard

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to monitor led to anoxic brain injury
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered severe anoxic brain injury and died

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to monitor ventilator
Causation established - failure to monitor led to anoxic brain injury and death

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper monitoring would have prevented anoxic brain injury

court_findings.legal_significance: Failure to monitor ventilator led to death
